seoTitle: Long Pocket vs Longreach — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Long Pocket and Longreach. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Long Pocket vs Longreach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Long Pocket and Longreach.
heading: Long Pocket vs Longreach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Long Pocket, Queensland and Longreach, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Longreach has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Long Pocket (31/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Long Pocket or Longreach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Long Pocket has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Longreach scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Long Pocket scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Longreach scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Long Pocket or Longreach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Longreach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
